title: Piquant Pan-Seared Thai Red Snapper Spicy Boat Noodles
description: A peppery, snappy Thai dish featuring red snapper, a crowd-pleaser in Southern Thai cuisine that is lacto-vegetarian-friendly, served in a boat noodle vessel on a lazy Susan spinner.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the red snapper fillets and cook for 3 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Remove the snapper from the skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil and heat until shimmering.
Add the Thai red curry paste and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly, until fragrant.
Add the fish sauce, lime juice, chili flakes, brown sugar, kaffir lime leaves, and Thai basil leaves to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked red snapper fillets back to the skillet and toss to coat with the spicy sauce.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened slightly.
Meanwhile, cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
To assemble the boat noodles, place a portion of the cooked noodles into each of 4-6 boat noodle vessels or small bowls.
Add a few pieces of the cooked red snapper to each vessel, followed by a spoonful of the spicy sauce and a sprinkle of chopped peanuts and bean sprouts.
Serve immediately, garnished with additional Thai basil leaves if desired.
Garnish with additional Thai basil leaves if desired.
